operations.value: People with impaired kidney function should not use high-dose magnesium supplements without clinical guidance because reduced excretion can cause hypermagnesemia; magnesium can also reduce absorption of tetracycline or quinolone antibiotics, bisphosphonates, and some other medicines, so dosing separation may be needed.
operations.notes: High-priority renal and medication-interaction warning. The interaction evidence applies to magnesium salts generally.

sources.title: An Update on Hypomagnesemia and Hypermagnesemia

notes: Adds the principal serious-risk and medication-separation guidance for oral magnesium products.
